title: Smart Contract Languages
description: 8 languages that compile to Plutus Core — pick the one that fits your team.

stops.title: Aiken: The Popular Choice
stops.narrative: [Aiken](node:aiken) is used by 75%+ of Cardano developers. Rust-inspired syntax, purpose-built for smart contracts. Compiles to [UPLC](node:plutus-core) with aggressive optimization. Auto-generates [CIP-57](node:cip-57) blueprints. [Minswap](node:minswap) V2 is written in Aiken.

stops.title: Plinth: The Haskell Original
stops.narrative: [Plinth](node:plinth) (formerly PlutusTx) is the original on-chain language. Write in a subset of Haskell, get the full GHC type system. Maintained by IntersectMBO. [Plutarch](node:plutarch) offers a lower-level Haskell eDSL for performance-critical validators — used by [Liqwid](node:liqwid).

stops.title: OpShin: Python for Cardano
stops.narrative: [OpShin](node:opshin) lets you write validators in 100% valid Python syntax. Strict typing enforced at compile time. Integrates with PyCardano for off-chain code. The lowest barrier to entry for Python developers.

stops.title: Marlowe: Financial Contracts
stops.narrative: [Marlowe](node:marlowe) is a DSL for financial contracts — non-Turing-complete by design, so contracts can be exhaustively analyzed and formally verified. Think loans, escrows, and swaps with mathematical guarantees. Now community-owned.

stops.title: The JS/TS Options
stops.narrative: [Helios](node:helios) and [plu-ts](node:plu-ts) target JavaScript/TypeScript developers. Helios has its own on-chain DSL plus off-chain tx building. plu-ts constructs UPLC AST directly in TypeScript. [Scalus](node:scalus) covers Scala developers.
